What Honeybook Does

HoneyBook is extremely popular among smaller agencies (1вЂ“10 employees). вЂ‹It focuses on "Clientflows", that is, it combines the proposal, the contract, and the first payment into one beautiful, interactive file.

Source: Honeybook

вЂ‹Key Features

вЂ‹рџЏ† Smart Files: Highly customizable documents that allow clients to select services and pay in one go.

вЂ‹рџЏ† Automated Scheduling: Integrated booking so clients can pay a retainer and book their kickoff call simultaneously.

рџЏ† Simple Invoicing: Very easy to set up recurring payments for monthly retainers.

вЂ‹Benefits of Honeybook

вЂ‹The biggest benefit is speed. You can go from a lead to a signed contract and paid deposit in minutes. For agencies selling "packaged" services, it makes the onboarding feel high-end.

вЂ‹Pricing

  • вЂ‹Starter: $36/mo
  • вЂ‹Essentials: $59/mo
  • вЂ‹Premium: $129/mo

All the plans start with a free trial. There are also significant discounts available on annual pricing. 

вЂ‹Pros

вЂ‹вњ… Highest-rated support for smaller teams.

вњ… The most "beautiful" client-facing interface on the market.

вњ… Templates that save hours of manual proposal drafting.

вЂ‹Cons

вЂ‹рџљ« Hard to manage a high volume of local business locations (no location-specific hierarchy).

рџљ« Limited advanced reporting for agency profitability.

вЂЌ
